diagrams.title: Midpoint Circle Algorithm - Pixel Selection
diagrams.description: Shows how the algorithm chooses between two candidate pixels (P1 and P2) based on whether the midpoint M lies inside or outside the true circle
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the decision process in choosing the better pixel approximation

diagrams.title: 8-Way Circle Symmetry
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how one computed point (x,y) is reflected to generate 7 other points using 8-way symmetry
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how computational work is reduced by factor of 8

diagrams.title: Ellipse Region 1 and Region 2
diagrams.description: Shows the ellipse divided into two regions based on slope, with the boundary where slope = 1
elements: Ellipse with semi-axes a and b
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why different pixel selection rules are needed for each region

diagrams.title: Aliasing Effect on Circle
diagrams.description: Compares a circle drawn with and without anti-aliasing to show the stair-stepping effect
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why anti-aliasing is important for visual quality
